methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $262M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $2.3B.

private.name: Exotec
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series D), post-money $2B

data.narrative:
Outrider, a Series D robotics and manufacturing automation company, has raised $262M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 private firms and 1 public company operating in the same sector, including peers at similar (Series C–E) funding stages: Exotec ($2.0B, Series D), Divergent ($2.3B, Series E), Hadrian ($1.6B, Series C), and Nimble Robotics ($1.0B, Series C).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a proxy for Series D value and applying a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical for advancement to the next stage, yields $393M. Peer-median analysis across the four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables produces a $1.8B implied valuation, with a range of $1.0B to $2.3B. The median of these two approaches is $1.1B.

Synthesizing both methods, the analysis supports a valuation range of $334M (low) to $2.1B (high), with a median point of $1.1B. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty regarding where Outrider sits within the peer distribution on execution metrics, market traction, and capital efficiency. Stage-progression suggests conservative downside; peer multiples suggest material upside potential. Reconciling this range hinges on granular assessment of revenue, unit economics, and competitive positioning relative to the named comparables.
